name: io.github.xieyuschen/gopls-mcp
description: Semantic code analysis for Go using gopls - language-aware navigation, definitions, references
author: xieyuschen

go_analyze_workspace.description: Analyze Go workspace structure, packages, and dependencies

go_get_started.description: Get beginner-friendly guide to start exploring Go projects

go_definition.description: Jump to definition of Go symbols using semantic location

go_implementation.description: Find interface implementations or types implementing interfaces

go_get_call_hierarchy.description: Trace call relationships - find callers and callees of functions

go_symbol_references.description: Find all usages/references of a symbol across the codebase

go_list_package_symbols.description: List all exported symbols (types, functions) in a Go package

go_list_module_packages.description: List all packages in a Go module

go_search.description: Search Go symbols by name with fuzzy matching

go_build_check.description: Check Go code for compilation and type errors

go_get_dependency_graph.description: Analyze dependencies between packages and modules

go_list_modules.description: List current module and direct dependencies only. Returns module paths only (no packages). By default, transitive dependencies are excluded. Set direct_only=false to show all dependencies including transitive ones. Use this to understand the module structure before exploring packages.

go_get_package_symbol_detail.description: Get detailed symbol information from a package. Returns Symbol objects with name, kind, signature, receiver (for methods), parent (for fields), documentation, and optional bodies. Symbol filters are REQUIRED - provide symbol_filters to retrieve specific symbols by name and receiver (e.g., [{name: "Start", receiver: "*Server"}]). For methods, receiver matching uses exact string match (e.g., "*Server" != "Server"). Use include_docs=true for documentation and include_bodies=true for function implementations. Use list_package_symbols to get all symbols in a package.

go_dryrun_rename_symbol.description: Preview a symbol rename operation across all files (DRY RUN - no changes are applied). Use go_symbol_references first to assess impact, then use this to preview the exact changes that would be made. Returns a unified diff showing all proposed modifications.

go_list_tools.description: List all available gopls-mcp tools with documentation and parameter schemas. These semantic analysis tools are more accurate (type-aware vs text matching), faster after warm-up (17x-587x per operation, breaks even after 3 queries), and save tokens compared to text-based search by leveraging gopls's cached type information.
